task_completion.description: Verify that a Builder class is implemented for SmartHomeDevice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In SmartHomeDevice.java, look for a nested static 'Builder' class (or a separate builder class/Lombok @Builder annotation) containing methods for setting the 7 fields (id, name, ipAddress, macAddress, firmwareVersion, location, isActive) and a 'build()' method.

task_completion.description: Verify that the original telescoping constructor in SmartHomeDevice is no longer public.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In SmartHomeDevice.java, the public constructor taking 7 arguments should be removed or made private. It should be replaced by a private constructor that takes the Builder as an argument.

task_completion.description: Verify that DeviceService.java uses the new Builder API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In DeviceService.java, the methods 'registerDefaultBulb' and 'createThermostat' should instantiate SmartHomeDevice using the builder pattern (e.g., 'SmartHomeDevice.builder().name(...).build()') instead of the 'new SmartHomeDevice(...)' constructor.

task_completion.description: Verify that DeviceServiceTest.java uses the new Builder API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In DeviceServiceTest.java, the test methods 'testManualCreation' and 'testServiceCreation' should instantiate SmartHomeDevice using the builder pattern instead of the 'new SmartHomeDevice(...)' constructor.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project compiles and all tests pass successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing the output of 'mvn test' with 'BUILD SUCCESS' and 'Tests run: 2, Failures: 0', or an IDE test runner window showing green checkmarks for the DeviceServiceTest class.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an actual IDE or text editor to modify the Java files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an editor like IntelliJ IDEA, VS Code, vim, or nano open with the Java files, rather than just using 'echo' or 'sed' commands blindly in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the tests were actually run and not just mocked or printed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The test output should be in a standard Maven or IDE test runner format, showing execution time and standard JUnit output, rather than a manually typed 'BUILD SUCCESS' string.

integrity.description: Verify the tests were not modified to trivially pass by removing assertions.
integrity.visual_evidence: DeviceServiceTest.java should still contain JUnit assertions (e.g., assertEquals, assertNotNull) checking the properties of the created devices.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/IdeaProjects/iot-device-manager. SmartHomeDevice has 7 fields: id, name, ipAddress, macAddress, firmwareVersion, location, isActive. DeviceService has 2 methods (registerDefaultBulb, createThermostat) that originally used the 7-parameter constructor. DeviceServiceTest has 2 test methods (testManualCreation, testServiceCreation). The project uses Maven and JUnit 5.9.2. The agent may use Lombok (@Builder) if they add the dependency, or manually implement the Builder pattern.
